data.title: Nvidia Stock vs. AMD Stock: What Is the Best AI Stock to Buy Right Now?
data.description:
The battle between Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has reached a fever pitch in 2026. While Nvidia continues to hold a dominant 85–90% market share in AI GPUs, AMD has spent the last year aggressively closing the gap with its Instinct MI450 series and a major multi-year partnership with OpenAI. For investors, the question is no longer who makes the better chip, but which stock has the most room to run at current valuations.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia: The Uncontested King of the Data Center

Nvidia remains the top choice for analysts who value stability and clear market leadership. Heading into its Fiscal Q4 earnings on February 25, 2026, analysts are expecting revenue to hit a staggering $65 billion.

  • The Bull Case: Demand for Nvidia's Blackwell and Rubin platforms is described by CEO Jensen Huang as "off the charts." Major tech giants like Meta (META) have recently signed multi-year deals for millions of Nvidia GPUs, proving that the world's biggest companies are still locked in to the Nvidia ecosystem.
  • Analyst Sentiment: Most analysts maintain a Buy rating, with the consensus landing at a Moderate Buy, with average price targets around $265, suggesting an upside of over 41%. They argue that Nvidia's competitive moat is simply too wide for anyone to cross in the next 12 to 18 months.

AMD: The Underdog with Massive Growth Potential

While Nvidia is the safe bet, many analysts are beginning to rotate into AMD, citing a more attractive valuation and explosive growth potential. AMD's stock has outperformed Nvidia in percentage returns over the last six months as it gains ground in the AI inference market.

  • The Bull Case: AMD recently secured a massive deal with OpenAI for its MI450 GPUs, which are set to deploy in the second half of 2026. Analysts point to AMD's lower P/E ratio and its massive addressable market, expected to hit $1 trillion by 2030, as reasons why the stock could be a wealth-builder by the end of the decade.
  • Analyst Sentiment: AMD is currently seen as a Strong Buy for those looking for a catch-up play. The average price target sits around $283, suggesting an upside of over 39%. Analysts believe that even small gains in GPU market share could lead to a massive surge in revenue for AMD because its AI business started from a much smaller base than Nvidia's.

Key Takeaway

Nvidia is like the gold standard. Everyone uses them, and they are incredibly profitable, but everyone already knows it. AMD is the determined challenger that is starting to win over big names like OpenAI and Microsoft. If you want a stock that will likely be steady and lead the market, analysts say stick with Nvidia. But if you are looking for a stock that has a better chance of growing your money faster because it's currently the underdog, AMD is the one Wall Street is watching.

Investors can compare both stocks side-by-side based on various financial metrics and analyst ratings on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Nvidia Stock Forecast: Will Q4 Earnings Justify the AI Rally?
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is set to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25, and the report may show if the recent AI rally can hold. The stock has risen about 34% over the past year on strong demand for its data-center chips. Investors now want clearer signs that this growth can continue. Bulls expect a strong outlook as Blackwell production ramps, while bears worry about margins, China export limits, supply constraints, and rising competition.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's a closer look at what bulls and bears are saying before the release.

What's Driving the Bullish Case Into Earnings?

1. AI Spending from Cloud Giants Remains Strong: Major cloud companies such as Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOGL) continue to raise AI infrastructure spending. In their latest earnings calls, each company highlighted plans to increase capital spending to build AI data centers and capacity. Bulls believe Nvidia remains the main supplier of AI accelerators, supporting continued data-center revenue growth.

2. Meta Partnership Adds Visibility: The recently expanded multiyear AI infrastructure partnership with Meta (META) signals sustained demand for large AI clusters. Bulls view this as evidence that hyperscaler investment in AI is not slowing. In a new note, Stifel kept a Buy rating and said the deal shows Meta's growing reliance on Nvidia's full-stack AI systems, including GPUs and networking.

3. Blackwell Ramp Could Drive the Next Leg Higher: Investors expect shipments of the new Blackwell GPUs to begin scaling this year. Bulls say the new platform should bring another upgrade cycle for customers and could extend Nvidia's growth into 2026.

4. Wall Street Analysts Remain Positive: Wall Street continues to rate the stock a Strong Buy, pointing to Nvidia's dominant position in AI computing and its software ecosystem advantage. Strong forward guidance, rather than the reported quarter itself, is seen as the key upside catalyst. UBS recently raised its price target to $245 and said supply-chain checks remain healthy, while Goldman expects another revenue and earnings beat driven by hyperscaler spending.

Why Some See Limited Upside

1. China Restrictions Could Affect Guidance: Nvidia's advanced GPUs remain subject to U.S. export controls to China, a market that previously contributed meaningful data-center revenue. Bears worry the company may exclude China sales from forward guidance, which could lower growth expectations even if the quarterly results are strong.

2. Investors Are Closely Watching Gross Margins: Rising high-bandwidth memory (HBM) costs, a critical component in AI accelerators, along with potential export-related expenses could push margins below the mid-70% range Wall Street expects. A margin decline would likely weigh on the stock.

3. Rising Competition from Cloud Customers: Major hyperscalers are increasingly developing their own AI chips and optimizing workloads. While Nvidia still dominates AI training, bears argue growing in-house solutions at large cloud companies could gradually limit future pricing power and growth.

4. High Expectations Leave Little Room for Error: After a massive multi-year rally, expectations are extremely high heading into earnings. Bears believe the stock may react more to forward guidance than reported results, meaning even a modestly cautious outlook could trigger volatility.

Key Numbers to Watch in Q4 Earnings

According to Wall Street estimates, Nvidia is expected to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are expected to jump 68% year-over-year to $65.67 billion.

For full-year fiscal 2026, sales are projected at $213.3 billion and adjusted earnings at $4.69 per share.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Metaplanet Stock Rises as CEO Silences the Critics and Denies Hiding Bitcoin Trade Details
data.description:
Metaplanet (JP:3350), the Japanese firm often compared to Strategy (MSTR), is currently at the center of a heated debate over its massive Bitcoin (BTC-USD) bets. As some investors express anger over the company's recent financial results, CEO Simon Gerovich has stepped in to set the record straight. Critics on social media accused the firm of hiding details about its Bitcoin-backed loans and "gambling" with shareholder money on risky trades. Gerovich fired back, stating that these claims are simply a misunderstanding of how the company reports its numbers. He insists that every move has been made public and that the company's foundation is stronger than ever. The market responded with immediate optimism, sending the stock price up 2.90% to ¥319.00 as investors regained confidence following the CEO's defense.

data.title: Temporary Power Firm Aggreko Could Light Up New York with $12B IPO
data.description:
Temporary power giant Aggreko may be ready to light up New York on reports that it could be eyeing up a $12 billion IPO.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

List or Sell Stake

According to an article in the Financial Times, the private equity owners of Aggreko are exploring options to either re-list the company in the U.S. later this year or sell a minority stake potentially valuing the business at between $10 billion and $12 billion. Goldman Sachs (GS) and JP Morgan (JPM) are reportedly the company's financial advisers.

Glasgow, Scotland-based Aggreko was listed in the U.K. before being bought by PE firms TDR Capital and iSquared Capital for about £2.3 billion in 2021.

Since then Aggreko has performed strongly with revenues rising from about £1.76 billion in 2022 to £2.2 billion in 2023. Analysts have forecast that the group is likely to have continued posting double-digit revenue growth of between 13% and 14% between 2025 and 2026. Adjusted EBITDA is forecast to have risen from around £530 million in 2022 to £720 to £750 million in 2023.

AI Power Demand

The business has also shifted away from smaller, higher‑risk projects and increased its emphasis on Europe, North America and the Middle East. Its work stretches across a range of sectors from supporting offshore wind farms to keeping ice rinks cold with hybrid chiller equipment, to providing temporary power at London theatres to ensure the show goes on for audiences.

If it is listed then it is likely to appeal to investors wanting to tap into growing demand for temporary power from the AI and digital infrastructure sectors. There is also more demand for temporary power from emerging markets, events and to provide support for under-strain national grids.

Potential buyers in the hunt for a minority stake could include the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, and PE rivals KKR (KKR) and CVC.

What Other IPOs are in the Pipeline?

Let's look at other IPOs in the pipeline with our TipRanks IPO calendar.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: QuantumScape Stock (QS) Drops Sharply After Earnings — Is the Selloff Overdone?
data.description:
QuantumScape (QS), the solid-state EV battery developer, fell sharply after its latest earnings report, which was released last week. The stock continued to trend higher in early trading on Friday after it rose about 0.2% on Thursday, ending a six-day losing streak. The bounce was small and came after heavy selling. The stock is now down roughly 32% year-to-date and about 30% over the past month.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earnings Beat Failed to Lift the Stock

The decline came after the company reported its quarterly results. QuantumScape reported a smaller-than-expected loss of $0.17 per share, slightly better than forecasts. However, the main concern remained unchanged, as the company still generated no meaningful revenue from its core battery business.

For now, the company is still in the development stage rather than a full operating business. It recorded about $19.5 million in customer billings last year and ended 2025 with roughly $971 million in cash. Management said its main focus is to prove the batteries can be produced at scale and used by automaker partners.

As a result, investors looked past the earnings beat and focused on the lack of actual product sales.

Wall Street's Take on QuantumScape

Analysts remain cautious on the stock, with all six covering analysts currently rating the shares Hold.

In a new research report, HSBC analyst Laisha Zaack upgraded the stock to Hold from Reduce with an $8.30 price target. She cited manufacturing progress, including the use of the faster Cobra production process, shipments of QSE-5 cells, installation of equipment for the Eagle production line, and new customer partnerships.

However, she still remains cautious. The company expects higher losses and has shared few details about its future plans, which has kept investor confidence weak.

Is QS Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on QuantumScape stock based on six unanimous Hold ratings assigned in the past three months. At $9.76, the average QS stock price target implies a 36.12% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Meta Platforms Trims Stock Awards as AI Investments Take Priority
data.description:
Social media giant Meta Platforms (META) has reduced its annual stock awards by about 5% for most of its employees, the Financial Times reported. The move comes as the company is prioritizing investments to support its artificial intelligence (AI) ambitions, including hiring top AI talent and building data centers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Cuts Stock Options for Second Consecutive Year Amid AI Focus

The 5% reported cut marks the second consecutive year that Meta Platforms has slashed its annual stock option distribution. Last year, the company lowered its equity-based awards by about 10%.

The reduction in this key component of employee compensation comes as CEO Mark Zuckerberg is directing massive investments toward AI infrastructure as the race to develop advanced models intensifies, with players like Alphabet-owned Google (GOOGL) and ChatGPT-maker OpenAI giving tough competition.

Notably, Meta Platforms expects 2026 capital expenditures in the range of $115 billion to $135 billion, nearly double the $72.2 billion spent last year. Given the massive investments, the company is focusing on driving efficiency to mitigate the impact on its profitability. For instance, in January 2026, Meta slashed about 10% of the jobs in its Reality Labs division.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Meta Platforms stock based on 40 Buys and four Hold recommendations. The average META stock price target of $856.11 indicates 33% upside potential. META stock is down 2% year-to-date.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is Opendoor Stock (OPEN) Rallying Today?
data.description:
